q: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one.
He who fears death will never do anything worthy of a man who is alive.
Man conquers the world by conquering himself.
The best revenge is not to be like your enemy.
It is not the man who has too little, but the man who craves more, that is poor.
Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants.
Don't explain your philosophy. Embody it.
If it is not right do not do it; if it is not true do not say it.
It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live.
Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
It's not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
Difficulties strengthen the mind, as labor does the body.
No man is free who is not master of himself.
When you arise in the morning think of what a privilege it is to be alive, to think, to enjoy, to love.
As is a tale, so is life: not how long it is, but how good it is, is what matters.
First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do.
Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, but do so with all your heart.
To be everywhere is to be nowhere.
The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
He is a wise man who does not grieve for the things which he has not, but rejoices for those which he has.
Life is long if you know how to use it.
Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them.
Better to trip with the feet than with the tongue.
If you want to improve, be content to be thought foolish and stupid.
Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself, in your way of thinking.
A gem cannot be polished without friction, nor a man perfected without trials.
Make the best use of what is in your power, and take the rest as it happens.
Reject your sense of injury and the injury itself disappears.
If a man knows not to which port he sails, no wind is favorable.
Man is not worried by real problems so much as by his imagined anxieties about real problems.
The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing.
Time discovers truth.
Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.
Everything we hear is an opinion, not a fact. Everything we see is a perspective, not the truth.
Only time can heal what reason cannot.
Know, first, who you are, and then adorn yourself accordingly.
Confine yourself to the present.
He who laughs at himself never runs out of things to laugh at.
Whatever happens to you has been waiting to happen since the beginning of time.
If you really want to escape the things that harass you, what you’re needing is not to be in a different place but to be a different person.
Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.
The best answer to anger is silence.
We are more often frightened than hurt; and we suffer more from imagination than from reality.
No great thing is created suddenly.
To live a good life: We have the potential for it. If we can learn to be indifferent to what makes no difference.
Associate with people who are likely to improve you.
Don't hope that events will turn out the way you want, welcome events in whichever way they happen: this is the path to peace.
The mind that is anxious about future events is miserable.
You could leave life right now. Let that determine what you do and say and think.
Wealth is the slave of a wise man, the master of a fool.
It is not events that disturb people, it is their judgements concerning them.
Look well into thyself; there is a source of strength which will always spring up if thou wilt always look.
True happiness is to enjoy the present, without anxious dependence upon the future.
Curb your desire—don’t set your heart on so many things and you will get what you need.
The universe is change; our life is what our thoughts make it.
Nothing, to my way of thinking, is a better proof of a well ordered mind than a man’s ability to stop just where he is and pass some time in his own company.
Keep silence for the most part, and speak only when you must, and then briefly.
Anger, if not restrained, is frequently more hurtful to us than the injury that provokes it.
Dig within. Within is the wellspring of Good; and it is always ready to bubble up, if you just dig.
A man who suffers before it is necessary, suffers more than is necessary.
It is impossible for a man to learn what he thinks he already knows.
When another blames you or hates you, or people voice similar criticisms, go to their souls, penetrate inside and see what kind of people they are.
Life is very short and anxious for those who forget the past, neglect the present, and fear the future.
Happiness and freedom begin with a clear understanding of one principle: Some things are within our control, and some things are not.
Your days are numbered. Use them to throw open the windows of your soul to the sun.
While we wait for life, life passes.
Don't seek for everything to happen as you wish it would, but rather wish that everything happens as it actually will—then your life will flow well.
There is only one way to happiness and that is to cease worrying about things which are beyond the power of our will.
When you wake up in the morning, tell yourself: The people I deal with today will be meddling, ungrateful, arrogant, dishonest, jealous, and surly.
Receive without conceit, release without struggle.
Whatever can happen at any time can happen today.
If evil be spoken of you and it be true, correct yourself, if it be a lie, laugh at it.
It is a rough road that leads to the heights of greatness.
Loss is nothing else but change, and change is Nature's delight.
If you wish to be loved, love.
First learn the meaning of what you say, and then speak.
Nothing is burdensome if taken lightly.
A man's worth is no greater than his ambitions.
It is the nature of the wise to resist pleasures, but the foolish to be a slave to them.
Ignorance is the cause of fear.
Think of the life you have lived until now as over and, as a dead man, see what’s left as a bonus and live it according to Nature.
The whole future lies in uncertainty: live immediately.
Know you not that a good man does nothing for appearance sake, but for the sake of doing right?
To be always happy is to be ignorant of half the nature of things.
Look back over the past, with its changing empires that rose and fell, and you can foresee the future, too.
Silence is a lesson learned through life's many sufferings.
Any person capable of angering you becomes your master.
How much time he gains who does not look to see what his neighbor says or does or thinks, but only at what he does himself.
Brave men rejoice in adversity, just as brave soldiers triumph in war.
Difficulty shows what men are.
Life is neither good nor evil, but only a place for good and evil.
Throw away your books; stop letting yourself be distracted.
Fire tests gold, suffering tests brave men.
If you want something good, get it from yourself.

To accuse others for one's own misfortune is a sign of want of education. To accuse oneself shows that one's education has begun. To accuse neither one's self nor others shows that one's education is complete.
We are members of one great body. Nature planted in us a mutual love and fitted us for a social life.
Be tolerant with others and strict with yourself.
